description_long: This object is a vintage portable reel-to-reel tape recorder housed in a hard, rectangular briefcase-style shell. The main body is a muted beige with a cream-colored top panel featuring two circular reel mounts, a central raised control section, and several knobs and switches. The sides include a vented grille, likely for a built-in speaker or cooling, and a sturdy, dark handle for carrying. The front panel has a socket array, possibly for power or audio connections, while the back features additional controls and a leather strap. The corners are slightly rounded, and the overall construction appears robust, with metallic hardware and visible fasteners.
description: A vintage beige and cream hard-shell briefcase with reel-to-reel tape recorder controls, vented sides, and a sturdy handle.
description_view_0: Front view showing a socket array and the cream top panel with reel mounts and control section.
description_view_1: Rear view highlighting the dark handle, leather strap, and additional control knobs.
description_view_2: Side view displaying a large vented grille and the cream top with control features.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with another vented grille, metallic hardware, and rounded corners.
attribute_handle_type: One fixed, rectangular, dark-colored handle made of leather or hard plastic, attached with metal brackets on the rear side; also a leather strap for additional carrying support.
attribute_lock_mechanism: Two visible metal latches on the top panel for securing the lid; no combination or key lock visible.
attribute_corner_reinforcements: None; corners are rounded but lack additional protective elements.

license_info.creator_display_name: Museum of Engineering and Technology, Krakow
